contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: dao_client validator rollout: capital_injection / dao_expenses / batch_qr_generator hardened
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/dao_client/pull/25
  
  Details:
  Follow-up to dao_client #24 (which added the validator/normalizer toolkit + wired it into the new report_currency_conversion module). This PR opts in the three highest-value existing modules — those where the worst nonsense slipped through silently before. Each is a few lines of validators={...} + normalizers={...} dict added to its build_event_cli call.
  
  report_capital_injection:
  - Ledger / Description: required (non-empty)
  - Ledger URL: google_sheets_url (strict — Capital Injection is managed-AGL-only; GAS rejects offchain via validateManagedLedger)
  - Amount: positive_number + normalize_number (strips commas; '1,000.50' -> 1000.50)
  
  report_dao_expenses:
  - DAO Member Name / Target Ledger / Inventory Type / Description: required
  - Inventory Quantity: positive_number + normalize_number
  - Latitude / Longitude: bounded by [-90,90] / [-180,180], empty allowed (geolocation is optional; some events log it, some don't)
  
  batch_qr_generator:
  - Was canonical_labels=[] — every caller had to use --attr 'Label=Value' with zero protection.
  - Now Currency + Quantity are first-class flags.
  - Currency: currency_code + normalize_currency (uppercases short ISO-like codes, preserves descriptive names)
  - Quantity: positive_integer (rejects 0, negatives, floats)
  
  Validator toolkit additions in validators.py (used by the three retrofits):
  - positive_integer (Quantity for batch QR)
  - latitude / longitude (lat/lng bounds with empty allowed)
  - google_sheets_url (strict — required + correct shape; complement to google_sheets_url_or_empty)
  
  Loosened currency_code validator after smoke test caught it rejecting 'Cacao Almonds (KG)' — the actual agroverse-inventory currency catalog has parens, commas, plus signs, etc. Now just rejects empty / control chars / non-letter-or-digit start. The DAO catalog is the source of truth; the validator just guards against obvious garbage.
  
  Documented one limitation in validators.py docstring: build_event_cli only runs validators on labels that received a value, so a missing --flag silently skips its validator (even 'required' ones). The receiving GAS catches truly-missing required fields. Closing this would require adding required_labels= to build_event_cli; tracked as future work.
  
  Smoke-tested 8 failure modes + 4 happy paths across the three modules; all errors return clean argparse parser.error output.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
